channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday signed into law a defense bill that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion despite his objections to language stripping coverage
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man faces murder charges in the death of a woman who was lit on fire in New York City subway
item.description: A man is facing murder and arson charges in New York City for allegedly setting a woman on fire inside a subway train and then watching her die after she was engulfed in flames, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Danny DeVito recalls spilling Coke on floor while attending Blazers game in 1975
item.description: Beloved Hollywood celebrity Danny DeVito appeared on former NFL wide receiver Julian Edelman’s podcast “Games with Names” on Dec. 3, where he shared a story about a night out in Portland with Jack Nicholson while the actors were filming “One Flew Over the Cuckoo’s Nest” in January of 1975.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Victim's father angered at 'despicable' timing of death sentence commutation
item.description: "I think President Biden offered a Christmas gift to the perpetrators of murder, but he offered only pain to the victims, the families of the victims," Tim Timmerman said.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Trial for suspect in second Trump assassination attempt delayed
item.description: Lawyers for the suspect, Ryan Routh, sought a delay of the scheduled Feb. 10 trial date, citing the large volume of evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mega Millions jackpot climbs to $970M ahead of Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot continues to grow ahead of Tuesday night’s drawing. The jackpot now stands at an estimated value of $970 million, California Lottery officials announced Monday. No one won the jackpot during Friday night’s drawing.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione appears in Manhattan court to face state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare is expected to be arraigned in a Manhattan court on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scott Peterson arrest months after Laci disappeared Christmas Eve may have been strategic: former homicide cop
item.description: Ted Williams explained why law enforcement officers don't always wait to find a body before bringing a murder charge, as in the case of missing Texas realtor Suzanne Simpson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua terrorizing Americans in at least 19 states
item.description: Tren de Aragua has a presence in at least 19 states as of December, which is up from 16 in November, indicating the gang's expansion within the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24 as Baker Mayfield and the Bucs lose control of their playoff fate
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Baker Mayfield came home to Texas looking to keep his Tampa Bay Buccaneers on track for the playoffs. Instead, they'll need some help.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drone mishap during Orlando holiday aerial show sends child to hospital
item.description: A 7-year-old child was struck by a drone that malfunctioned and crashed with several other drones during an aerial show in Orlando, Florida on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fireworks, multiple knives, pepper spray found in woman's carry-on bag at LAX
item.description: The TSA described the discovery as "shocking," even to its most tenured LAX officer, and said it was a prime example of all the things not to bring when traveling through the nation's airports.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 174 Colorado skiers and snowboarders rescued after a lift cracks
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — Officials were investigating Sunday what caused a crack in a Colorado ski lift that forced the evacuation of over 170 stranded skiers and snowboarders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KRON4

item.title: Missouri company creates healthy dog treats using invasive fish
item.description: A Missouri has found a way to turn an invasive fish into multiple useful products, including a new line of dog treats.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KRON4

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck through Texas mall, injuring 5
item.description: Authorities said the driver traveled "several hundred yards" through a JCPenney, injuring multiple people.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, Fox 5

item.title: Mystery drones could be identified faster using new detection tool, but FAA lacks resources
item.description: The FAA's new "digital license plate" for drones has yet to be rolled out to law enforcement, hindering efforts to identify mystery drones in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, KTLA 5

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KRON4

item.title: Former San Diego Padres player Rickey Henderson dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Former NFL star Michael Vick officially hired by Norfolk State on Friday
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Norfolk State officially named Michael Vick its head football coach Friday, the latest hire of a talented NFL player with no college coaching experience to lead an HBCU program.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.” On eight consecutive nightfalls, Jews gather with family and friends to light one additional candle in the menorah — a multibranched candelabra.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
